authorization.scope: narrow Trash safety correction discovered after compaction planning and before garbage-collection mutation

discovered_gaps.authorization: The frozen generation-ID command cannot carry itemized confirmation or verified-backup proof for non-regenerable artifacts.
discovered_gaps.execution: The contract did not freeze the trash descendant layout, retained-object subtraction, bounded retry, cancellation point of no return, or same-descriptor maintenance upgrade.
discovered_gaps.fault: Every existing fault would mislabel a valid non-regenerable selection; capability denial must remain typed and visible.
discovered_gaps.risk: An implementation could delete shared or non-regenerable data, self-contend on its own maintenance lease, or return from an unsynced partial quarantine.

correction.selection: Trash accepts a nonempty duplicate-free generation selection bounded by recovery_candidates_max. Fresh preflight must prove every active selection is currently orphaned; an absent active generation is accepted only when its exact canonical file already exists in trash as a retry. PlanCompaction is review input, never authorization.
correction.non_regenerable: Every selected generation must declare zero non-regenerable artifacts. Otherwise reject before mutation with ConfirmationRequired. The current API cannot satisfy that fault; a boolean is never itemized confirmation or verified-backup proof.
correction.retained_closure: Retain the union of every active unselected generation's physical-object closure, including roots and unselected orphans. Move only selected generation files and the selected closure minus that retained closure. Never sweep anonymous unrooted objects or move an object shared with a retained generation.
correction.maintenance: Require a confirmed writer and cancellably upgrade the actor's own held shared maintenance descriptor to exclusive. A failed nonblocking conversion must restore shared ownership before waiting, cancellation, or return. Fresh preflight and mutation occur only while exclusive; every exit restores shared ownership. Loss of the maintenance lease suspends the session and fails indeterminately.
correction.preflight: Before the first mutation, perform one complete bounded active-plus-trash inventory and validate namespaces, regular single-link types, canonical generation identity and bytes, refs/pins/graph, declared object lengths, selection, capacity/fan-out, existing destinations, and the deterministic move plan. Unknown, malformed, linked, or mismatched entries fail closed. Hash an existing trash collision only when needed to prove an exact retry.
correction.order: Process selected generation files in generation-ID order before movable objects in digest order. Each durable batch contains at most gc_batch_entries_max namespace mutations and gc_batch_bytes_max checked source-file bytes. Sync every affected active and trash parent directory in deterministic order before the batch is complete.
correction.retry: Never overwrite. Active exact plus trash absent uses no-replace rename; active absent plus trash exact is already quarantined. When both exact files exist and the active file is unretained, sync the trash file before removing the active duplicate. Both absent or any type, link, length, identity, or digest mismatch fails closed. A completely quarantined exact selection is a successful zero-work retry.
correction.cancellation: Lease waiting, preflight, and every pre-batch boundary are cancellable. From a batch's first directory creation, rename, or duplicate removal through all of that batch's required directory syncs, defer cancellation. Between batches cancellation may return Cancelled with only a durable retryable quarantine prefix; Cancelled therefore means a safe boundary, not necessarily zero maintenance progress, and never returns with an unsynced mutation.
correction.fault_semantics: Add only ConfirmationRequired and generalize Cancelled from pre-authority cancellation to cancellation at a safe boundary. Non-maintenance operations retain their existing no-authority-change behavior; Trash may report Cancelled after a fully synced retryable batch prefix.
correction.indeterminate: Any non-cancellation failure after the operation's first mutation, including a required directory-sync failure, returns CommitIndeterminate, suspends writes, performs no rollback, and requires close/reopen plus fresh inspection. A failure before the first mutation changes no bytes.
correction.diagnostics: On success, published_objects counts regular files newly removed from active storage and durably present in trash; streamed_bytes counts only bytes actually streamed for exact collision checks. Neither is a reclaim estimate.
correction.future_authorization: Supporting Trash or Purge for any non-regenerable artifact requires a separate owner-approved API correction carrying opaque snapshot-bound authorization over exact generation and artifact identities plus verified-backup proof.

superseded_prior_claims: maintenance-transition-correction unchanged: commands, completions, faults, and limits; faults gain ConfirmationRequired and Cancelled gains safe-boundary semantics
maintenance-transition-correction unchanged: persisted paths and bytes; active bytes remain unchanged while exact trash descendants are added
maintenance-transition-correction unchanged: lease, compaction, and purge policy; Trash lease and compaction policy are specialized while Purge remains unchanged
unchanged: the sixteen public root exports and every command and completion shape
active envelope, ref, generation, object, and fixture bytes
no backup, reclaim, durability, public, or product claim
evidence: exact architecture binding and normalized-contract checks
same-descriptor lease transition and restoration tests
required future generated zero-declared-non-regenerable Trash, retry, sharing, cancellation, contention, and failure-injection tests
unchanged public root, dependency, fixture, and active-wire guards
stop_condition: Any other public API change, current proof-bearing non-regenerable authorization, active persisted-byte change, dependency, fixture, product wiring, Purge implementation, or durability claim must stop for separate review.
rollback: revert this correction before Trash implementation; the project store remains off-product
